body_txt: This trope is about yelling a ridiculously long, loud "WHAAAAAT!?" usually uttered when the character is confused, annoyed or just plain ol' shocked. May be used in the form of: "You WHAT?" The ubiquitous Japanese equivalent is a Big "NANI?!" or Big "NANDA?!" / "NANDE?!" note In some cases, it can be rendered as a Big "NANDATTE?!"/"NANDATTO?!" / other variants. — often translated as "Say what?" or "What the—" to match the two-syllable format. However, it's also quite common to see it as a Big "EEEEH?!" A variant appears where a character, in response to an ally's foolish or incredulous action, utters a "You WHAT?!/You did WHAT?!" such as when a villain finds out his top minion had the hero on the ropes, then let them get away. If it gets cut off, it's more likely to be a Flat "What" for some reason. A Sub-Trope of Big Word Shout . Compare Flat "What" , Defensive "What?" , Big "NO!" , Big "WHY?!" .
